work: Calzavara et al., Site Policy, NDSS 2021
what_it_does: Formalizes inconsistencies for cookies, CSP, and HSTS and measures them across sites.
overlap: Browser-enforced policies; formalization of policy inconsistency.
our_boundary: We study developer-facing explicit intent, framework/generated policy surfaces, and minimal source-grounded witnesses; no live crawling or origin-policy proposal.

what_it_does: Generates CSP/HTML tests to find browser CSP enforcement bugs via differential testing.
our_boundary: We do not search for browser implementation bugs; browser semantics are the reference relation for policy-engineering drift.

what_it_does: Defines formal CSP semantics and performs large-scale CSP deployment analysis.
overlap: Formal CSP semantics and deployment effectiveness.
our_boundary: We generalize beyond CSP and deployment prevalence to cross-policy/framework intent drift and source-grounded witness minimization.

what_it_does: Measures CSP effectiveness in the wild.
our_boundary: We avoid site-scanning prevalence and target reproducible policy-engineering semantics.

what_it_does: Shows many real CSP whitelists are bypassable and motivates nonce/strict-dynamic guidance.
overlap: CSP security relevance and nonce motivation.
our_boundary: We focus on how developer/framework intent around nonces and delivery/fallback becomes browser-effective or drifts.

what_it_does: Generates CSPs for real sites via black-box rewriting.
our_boundary: We are not generating policies for sites; we model and witness semantic drift across policy families.

work: Calzavara et al., CCSP, USENIX Security 2017
what_it_does: Proposes runtime composition to relax CSP.
our_boundary: We use composition as an analysis dimension for existing browser/framework policies rather than proposing a CSP extension.

what_it_does: Studies CORS design, implementation, deployment and developer misunderstanding.
our_boundary: CORS is one stratum in a cross-policy source-grounded intent-drift model; no live web measurement.

what_it_does: Checks security-relevant headers and reports scanner-style results.
our_boundary: We treat header scanners as baselines/controls; core oracle reasons about effective semantics and explicit intent.

what_it_does: Reviews CSP for XSS mitigation and bypass-oriented concerns.
our_boundary: We do not equate bypass-oriented CSP warnings with cross-policy intent drift; mapping is coarse and declared.

work: CSP Level 3; Fetch; RFC 6797; HTML/Permissions Policy docs
what_it_does: Defines browser-policy mechanisms and their delivery/effective behavior.
our_boundary: We encode only fragments with source-grounded obligations and mark unsupported behaviors unknown.

work: Next.js, Helmet, Django, Spring Security, Rails, Express, ASP.NET Core docs
what_it_does: Developer-facing policy APIs, defaults, and migration guidance.
our_boundary: We admit only explicit source claims and do not infer private deployment intent.
